name: legacy-safe
description: Legacy-Safe: characterize-before-change, golden tests, test-after-remediation allowed for existing code.

rules.guidance: Before modifying legacy code, write characterization tests that document current (possibly incorrect) behavior. These tests prevent unintended regression during refactoring.
Use golden (snapshot) tests for complex legacy output that is hard to assert inline. Golden tests must be reviewed and updated intentionally, not auto-accepted.
For existing untested legacy code, adding tests after the fact is permitted and encouraged. New behavior added to legacy code still requires tests-first.
Replace legacy components incrementally using the Strangler Fig pattern. Tests must pass at every step of the replacement. Do not rewrite and swap in one large change.
